---
title: "Capstone"
description: "Plan, build, test, document, and present a beginner-friendly Python project that integrates the course."
execute:
  echo: true
---

## Why this chapter matters

Plan, build, test, document, and present a beginner-friendly Python project that integrates the course.

Lessons are now grouped by coherent learner topics. A page may contain multiple
short quizzes when the page has multiple sections, so students can check one
idea before moving to the next.

::: {.questions}
### Questions

- Which lessons belong in this chapter?
- What should I review before moving on?
- How do the lesson sections build one practical skill?
:::

## Lesson path

- [Project Selection and Planning](project-selection-and-planning.qmd) — Choose a small capstone and break it into realistic milestones.
- [Testing and README](testing-and-readme.qmd) — Protect capstone behavior with tests and explain the project clearly.
- [Presentation and Rubric](presentation-and-rubric.qmd) — Present the project story and evaluate it with visible criteria.

## Chapter checkpoint

::: {.quiz-box}
Use this short checkpoint before starting the chapter.
:::

<script type="application/json" class="fcpython-ojs-quiz-config">
{
  "id": "capstone-overview",
  "title": "Capstone overview checkpoint",
  "instructions": "Choose an answer, then check your work.",
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "chapter-purpose",
      "prompt": "What is the purpose of the Capstone chapter?",
      "options": [
        "Plan, build, test, document, and present a beginner-friendly Python project that integrates the course.",
        "To memorize disconnected commands",
        "To skip practice",
        "To avoid debugging"
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Plan, build, test, document, and present a beginner-friendly Python project that integrates the course."
    },
    {
      "id": "study-flow",
      "prompt": "Why can a lesson contain multiple quizzes?",
      "options": [
        "Each quiz checks one section before the next section builds on it.",
        "Quizzes replace examples.",
        "Multiple quizzes mean the topic is unrelated.",
        "Quizzes should only appear at the end of the course."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Section-level quizzes help learners review while the idea is still fresh."
    }
  ]
}
</script>

{{< include ../_includes/ojs-quiz.qmd >}}
